sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 34

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 34:1
items.html:
(to the question) Hey Damim Tehorim [five pure bloods]
Not all the discernments of MAN [Mayin Nukvin] that rose in the secret of the Zivug of Ibur [conception] can be clarified there, for necessarily there are parts whose time of clarification has not yet arrived, but which must be clarified through the Lights of Yenika [suckling] or through the Lights of Gadlut [greatness]. Therefore their roots lie there in a still state — that is, they do not join with the Lights and vessels of the newborn. And there are measures in them: part of them turn into milk and are clarified through the Mochin of Yenika; and there are parts in them whose correction is still altogether distant, and they must leave there entirely and return to the outer ones [Chitzonim], as these measures are explained in their place. Therefore the whole of them divides into two discernments: the first are the five impure bloods, and the second are the five pure bloods. Their division follows the roots of the two kinds of MAN that are there in the Yesod of the Nukva. For there are MAN received by her from ZA from the time of Katnut [smallness], coming mainly from the discernment of the Masach [screen] of NHY of AK, from the secret of the Vav and the point that it imparted to the Yesodot of AVI of Nekudim, which are the roots of ZON, as is known. And the second MAN are what she received from Ima, coming from the discernment of the Masach of SAG of AK, from the secret of the Light of the looking of the eyes [Histaklut Eynaim] in Nekudim, from which only the first three Partzufim of Atzilut were made; and they are called the discernments of the second MaNTzePaCh, which she received not through ZA but from Ima. From these, no refuse is strained off such as would return and clothe in the outer ones, because they are discernments of vessels of Bina — for SAG is entirely the discernment of Bina — and therefore, after they have been clarified or have received some correction, it is never again lost from them, since from their root they are without any Tzimtzum [restriction] at all. Not so the first MaNTzePaCh of ZA, which are in essence from the discernment of the lower Hey; although they received in them from the discernment of the upper Hey, in the secret of the joining of the quality of mercy with judgment, still, so long as they have not received their complete clarification, they can be spoiled and fall back into the Klipot [shells], in the discernment of the five impure bloods. (p. 781, Item 74; and p. 786, Item 80)
